meta.title: AWS Web Application
meta.description: Typical AWS stack serving desktop and mobile clients through a load balancer, Nginx reverse proxy, PHP and Node.js services, RDS database, and Elasticsearch.
Desktop browsers and mobile apps sending HTTPS requests.
VPC with public and private subnets across two availability zones.
Application Load Balancer + Nginx layer. Terminates TLS and routes by path.
PHP-FPM for page rendering, Node.js for API and real-time routes. Auto-scaled EC2 instances in private subnets.
Persistent storage: RDS PostgreSQL (Multi-AZ) and Elasticsearch (OpenSearch).
Standard browser client. Loads the React SPA and makes REST/GraphQL calls.
iOS / Android native app. Uses the same REST API as the desktop client.
AWS Application Load Balancer. Terminates TLS (ACM cert), forwards HTTP/2 to Nginx target group.
meta.notes: Idle timeout: 60 s. Cross-zone load balancing enabled.

meta.description: Reverse proxy layer. Routes /api/* and /ws/* to Node.js; everything else to PHP-FPM. Serves static assets from S3 via signed URLs.
meta.notes: Rate limiting: 200 req/s per IP. Gzip compression enabled.

meta.description: Laravel application server. Handles SSR page requests, form submissions, and admin routes.
meta.notes: PHP 8.3, Laravel 11. OPcache enabled. Auto-scaled: 2–10 instances.

meta.description: Express REST + WebSocket server. Handles /api/* routes and real-time features (notifications, live updates).
meta.notes: Node 20 LTS. PM2 cluster mode: 4 workers per instance. Auto-scaled: 2–8 instances.

meta.description: PostgreSQL 16, db.r6g.2xlarge, Multi-AZ deployment. Primary in us-east-1a, standby in us-east-1b.
meta.notes: Automated backups: 7-day retention. Storage auto-scaling up to 1 TB.

meta.description: AWS OpenSearch Service (Elasticsearch-compatible). 3-node cluster for full-text product and content search.
meta.notes: Index: products, articles. Refresh interval: 1 s. Replicas: 1.

steps.name: Overview
steps.title: AWS Web Application Overview
steps.description: Desktop and mobile clients reach the application through an ALB and Nginx reverse proxy. PHP-FPM handles page rendering; Node.js handles the API and real-time traffic. Both services share RDS PostgreSQL and Elasticsearch.

steps.name: Client request
steps.title: Client sends HTTPS request
steps.description: Desktop and mobile clients initiate HTTPS requests to the application's domain. The ALB is the single entry point — no client connects directly to app servers.

steps.name: ALB routes
steps.title: ALB terminates TLS and forwards
steps.description: The Application Load Balancer terminates the TLS connection (using an ACM certificate), then forwards plain HTTP/2 to the Nginx target group.

annotations.text: TLS termination → HTTP/2 forward to Nginx target group

steps.name: Nginx routes
steps.title: Nginx decides: PHP or Node?
steps.description: Nginx inspects the request path. Requests to /api/* and /ws/* are proxied to the Node.js service; everything else (page requests, form POSTs) goes to PHP-FPM via FastCGI.

annotations.text:
location /api/ → proxy_pass node:3000
location / → fastcgi_pass php:9000

steps.name: Page request → PHP
steps.title: PHP-FPM handles page render
steps.description: A page request (e.g. /products/42) is passed to the Laravel application via FastCGI. PHP builds the HTML response, querying the database as needed.

steps.name: PHP queries DB
steps.title: PHP reads from RDS
steps.description: Laravel's Eloquent ORM issues a SQL SELECT to RDS PostgreSQL via the PDO driver over the private subnet. The result is used to render the product page.

data.query: SELECT p.*, i.url FROM products p LEFT JOIN images i ON i.product_id = p.id WHERE p.id = $1

steps.name: API request → Node
steps.title: API call handled by Node.js
steps.description: An API request (e.g. GET /api/v1/search?q=shoes) is proxied to the Node.js service, which validates auth, parses params, and queries Elasticsearch.

steps.name: Node queries Elasticsearch
steps.title: Node.js issues search query
steps.description: The Node.js service constructs an Elasticsearch multi-match query and sends it to the OpenSearch cluster over the private subnet.

annotations.text: esClient.search({ index: 'products', query: { multi_match: { query: 'shoes', fields: ['name^3', 'description'] } } })

steps.name: Node writes to DB
steps.title: Node.js persists data to RDS
steps.description: For write operations (e.g. creating a cart item or recording an event), Node.js uses the pg driver to run a parameterised INSERT or UPDATE against RDS PostgreSQL.

data.query: INSERT INTO cart_items (user_id, product_id, qty) VALUES ($1,$2,$3)

steps.name: Full request cycle
steps.title: End-to-end request at scale
steps.description: At full load both clients fire concurrent requests. The ALB distributes traffic across the Nginx fleet. PHP and Node scale independently based on CPU utilisation. RDS serves both services; Elasticsearch handles search for both.
